name: what3words Address Encoding API
what_it_does: Proprietary location encoding system with REST API — enabling developers to convert GPS coordinates to memorable 3-word addresses (///filled.count.soap) and vice versa, providing a precision location system for any 3m×3m square on Earth, with applications in delivery, emergency services, logistics, and addressing in areas without street addresses.
use_cases: Converting GPS coordinates to 3-word addresses via what3words API for delivery precision
Resolving 3-word addresses to coordinates via what3words API for navigation and dispatch
Integrating what3words into checkout flows via API for delivery address precision
Building emergency service location tools via what3words API for precise caller location
Enabling address entry via what3words in logistics platforms for rural delivery accuracy
not_for: Standard street address geocoding (use Google Maps Geocoding or Mapbox for traditional addresses)
Building a maps platform without premium plan for commercial use
Teams wanting open-source/open-standard location encoding (what3words is proprietary)
best_when: A delivery company, emergency service, or logistics platform needs precise location communication for areas with poor addressing — or a memorable, shareable location format that works globally in multiple languages.
avoid_when: You need traditional street address geocoding, want open-standard addressing, or need offline use without SDK licensing.

auth.notes: API key from what3words developer account. key parameter in query string. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. No webhooks. SDKs for 8+ languages. Freemium API access. Commercial licensing for production use. API key identifies the plan tier and rate limits.

pricing.paid_tiers: Startup from $10/month; Business $199/month; Enterprise custom; Offline SDK separate
pricing.notes: UK company (London). Founded 2013 by Chris Sheldrick, Jack Waley-Cohen, and Mohan Ganesalingam. Raised $50M+ (SoftBank, Intel Capital). Proprietary encoding algorithm — not open source. Every 3m×3m square on Earth has a unique 3-word address. 59 languages supported. Used by: DHL, Volkswagen, Mercedez-Benz, UN, emergency services in 190+ countries. Mongolia official postal system uses what3words. UK emergency services (999) integration. Not open-source — algorithm is proprietary IP. Mobile SDKs for offline use require commercial license. Growing adoption in e-commerce logistics for delivery precision.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with API key via query param. Good documentation. SDKs for 8+ languages. No webhooks. GDPR compliant. Freemium (1,000 free/month). Proprietary algorithm — no offline without licensed SDK. Simple coordinate-to-words and words-to-coordinate conversion.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All API calls are fully idempotent — same coordinates always return same 3-word address. Same 3-word address always returns same coordinates. No state mutations — pure encoding/decoding.
known_agent_gotchas: Proprietary algorithm — cannot use offline without licensed offline SDK (additional cost)
API key in query string — ensure HTTPS only (key visible in URL logs)
1,000 requests/month free — insufficient for production; upgrade required
Commercial use restrictions — review terms for revenue-generating applications
Language support varies — verify language availability for target market

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. API key via query string — use HTTPS only. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Location data (coordinates) is sensitive — handle with appropriate privacy controls. GDPR data minimization — don't store what3words addresses with PII unnecessarily. UK company with GDPR awareness. Proprietary algorithm — supply chain risk if what3words changes terms.
